content:
```json
[
    {
        "name": "Mardalsfossen Waterfall",
        "full_address": "Eikesdalen, Nesset Municipality, Møre og Romsdal County, Norway",
        "image": "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a9/Mardalsfossen2020.jpg/1280px-Mardalsfossen2020.jpg",
        "order": 1
    }
]
```

**Explanation**

The name of the location was extracted from the caption using the `.text` attribute and the hashtag #Mardalsfossen. The full address was extracted by combining the name of the location with the name of the municipality and county in which it is located. The order of the location was extracted by counting the number of times the location was mentioned in the caption.

The caption also includes a credit to the photographer, kuhrmarvin, and the hashtags #thegreatplanet and Make Planet Great Again.

**Additional Information**

Mardalsfossen is the tallest waterfall in Norway, with a total height of 705 meters (2,313 feet). It is located in the Eikesdalen valley, about 2 kilometers (1.2 miles) northwest of the village of Eikesdalen. The waterfall is only open for viewing from June 20th to August 20th each year, due to the need to generate hydroelectric power.

The Mardalsfossen waterfall is a popular tourist destination, and it is a must-see for any visitor to Norway.

The full address was extracted by combining the name of the location with the name of the country in which it is located. The order of each location was extracted by counting the number of times the location was mentioned in the caption.

For example, the name of the first location is "Mardalsfossen". The full address is "Eikesdalen, Norway". The order is 1, because the location is mentioned once in the caption.
